businessSummary.oneLiner: Government and enterprise AI data analytics platform leader with Gotham (defense), Foundry (enterprise), and AIP (AI Platform)
businessSummary.description: Palantir builds AI-powered data analytics platforms for government and enterprise clients. FY2025 revenue hit $4.48B (+56%), with US commercial revenue surpassing $500M, proving successful expansion into the private sector. The recent addition of Keel to the Navy ShipOS project extended defense AI applications to submarine production. The stock rose 1,860% over 3 years but experienced 10+ drawdowns of 20%+ during that period, making it a high-volatility name.

keyMetrics.name: P/E (TTM)
keyMetrics.interpretation: 10x the S&P 500 average of 22x. Extreme premium persists even after pullback. War could boost defense AI spending

keyMetrics.name: Forward P/E
keyMetrics.interpretation: Based on FY2026 EPS of $1.34. War-benefit expectations sustain the premium

keyMetrics.name: P/S (Price-to-Sales)
keyMetrics.interpretation: Extreme vs SaaS average of 10-15x, but 60%+ growth rate attempts to justify it

keyMetrics.name: Revenue Growth
keyMetrics.interpretation: FY2025 $4.48B, FY2026 guidance $7.18-7.20B (+60%). US commercial revenue +115% outlook

keyMetrics.name: Operating Margin
keyMetrics.interpretation: Top-tier among SaaS companies. Simultaneous high profitability and growth

keyMetrics.name: FCF Margin
keyMetrics.interpretation: FCF $2.1B. Best-in-class cash generation in the software industry

growthDrivers.title: Iran War $200B Pentagon Budget: Defense AI Demand Surge
growthDrivers.description: Pentagon requested $200B for the Iran war. Escalation drives surging demand for military AI, intelligence analysis, and logistics automation. Palantir's Gotham, Maven, and ShipOS are all direct beneficiaries.

growthDrivers.title: Moder AI Mortgage Partnership: Financial Services Expansion
growthDrivers.description: Co-developing an AI mortgage operations platform with Freedom Mortgage as the first pilot. Proves commercial expansion beyond defense into financial services.

growthDrivers.title: Mizuho: 'No Comparable in Software' - Outperform $195 Reiterated
growthDrivers.description: Mizuho reiterated Outperform after executive meeting, calling Palantir 'a company with no comparable in the software industry.'

growthDrivers.title: UBS $200 Upgrade + Wedbush $230 Maintained
growthDrivers.description: UBS Karl Keirstead raised target from $180 to $200 (+33%). Wedbush maintains $230 Buy.

growthDrivers.title: Navy ShipOS $448M: AI Applied to Submarine Production
growthDrivers.description: Keel joined Palantir on the Navy ShipOS project, applying AI to submarine production lines.

growthDrivers.title: Gartner: Global AI Spending $2.5T Forecast
growthDrivers.description: Gartner forecasts 2026 global AI spending at $2.5T (+44% YoY). Structural tailwind for AI platform leaders like Palantir.

competitiveAdvantage.summary: 20+ years of government and defense AI track record. Security clearances create insurmountable entry barriers. ShipOS extends AI coverage to military production.

moats.description: Highest security clearances from DoD and intelligence agencies. Competitors cannot enter.
AI deployed in submarine production and naval shipyards. Expanding beyond battlefield to all military production.
Sovereign AI OS + NVIDIA hardware ecosystem. Positioning as standard for government AI infrastructure worldwide.

competitors.name: CrowdStrike
competitors.detail: Cybersecurity AI. Motley Fool compared both as AI beneficiary stocks

competitors.name: Microsoft (Azure AI)
competitors.detail: Cloud AI platform. General-purpose approach vs Palantir's specialized analytics

competitors.name: C3.ai
competitors.detail: Enterprise AI. Lags Palantir in scale and track record

recentNews.headline: Iran War Ground Escalation: Pentagon Requests $200B War Budget
recentNews.significance: War escalation means increased defense AI spending. Palantir's Gotham, Maven, and ShipOS are all direct beneficiaries. However, broader market selloff dragged PLTR down -3.25%.

recentNews.headline: Mizuho Reiterates Outperform $195 After Executive Meeting
recentNews.significance: 'No comparable in software' assessment. Focused on speed of commercial AI pilot conversions to paid.

recentNews.headline: Motley Fool: Palantir vs CrowdStrike as AI Beneficiaries
recentNews.significance: Both classified as AI beneficiary stocks. Palantir leads in data analytics, CrowdStrike in cybersecurity.

recentNews.headline: Moder AI Mortgage Platform Partnership Announced
recentNews.significance: Freedom Mortgage pilot. Proves AI expansion beyond defense into financial services.

recentNews.headline: Pentagon Pushing to Remove Anthropic Claude Within 6 Months
recentNews.significance: DoD seeking to remove Anthropic Claude from Maven within 6 months, but military users resist replacement. Uncertainty around Maven AI engine transition.

analystOpinions.notableComment: UBS $200 (+33%), Wedbush $230 (+53%), Mizuho $195 reiterated. 16 of 28 analysts rate Buy. Iran war $200B budget could surge defense AI demand, maintaining defense premium.

risks.title: Extreme Valuation: P/E 221x, P/S 77x
risks.description: 10x the S&P 500 average of 22x. FCF yield below 1%. Valuation burden persists despite war-benefit expectations.

risks.title: Below Both 50-Day and 200-Day Moving Averages: Technical Weakness
risks.description: Current price $150.68 is below both SMA-50 ($152.19) and SMA-200 ($163.34). Watch for potential death cross. Short-term technical picture has deteriorated.

risks.title: Iran War Prolongation Recession Risk
risks.description: Pentagon $200B budget favors defense, but Hormuz blockade and oil price surge could trigger recession, sharply cutting civilian IT spending. A double-edged sword as Palantir's commercial revenue share grows.

risks.title: Continued Insider Selling
risks.description: CEO Alex Karp sold $6.6M, Stephen Cohen sold $43.74M. Management actively taking profits at current prices.

risks.title: Swiss Lawsuit + NHS Controversy: European Risk
risks.description: Guardian reported Swiss lawsuit as 'threat campaign.' NHS largest hospital group chairman resigned. European public contract risk intensifying.

risks.title: Share Dilution
risks.description: Stock-based compensation (SBC) dilutes approximately 5% annually. Structural drag on long-term returns.

risks.title: Pentagon Anthropic Claude Removal Disruption
risks.description: Maven AI engine replacement involves costs and time. Short-term uncertainty.

buyReasons.title: Iran War $200B: Structural Increase in Defense AI Demand
buyReasons.rationale: As war escalates, demand for military AI, intelligence analysis, and logistics automation surges. Palantir is the most direct beneficiary.

buyReasons.title: Holding Above 20-Day SMA ($146): Short-Term Support Confirmed
buyReasons.rationale: Below 50-day and 200-day SMAs but holding above 20-day SMA ($146.87). RSI at 57, neutral not oversold. Room for rebound.

buyReasons.title: Moder Mortgage + 3 Analyst Upgrades: Growth Story Intact
buyReasons.rationale: TAM expansion via financial services entry. UBS/Wedbush/Mizuho all maintain bullish stance. Consensus target $196 (+30%).

buyReasons.title: Rule of 40 Score 127%: Growth + Profitability
buyReasons.rationale: Companies achieving 60% revenue growth and 31% operating margin simultaneously are extremely rare. Structural beneficiary of Gartner's $2.5T AI market.

overallOpinion: The Iran war $200B budget request is structurally expanding defense AI demand. Gotham, Maven, and ShipOS are all direct beneficiaries, and Mizuho called Palantir 'incomparable in software.'
Technically, the stock is below both the 50-day SMA ($152) and 200-day SMA ($163). It's holding above the 20-day SMA ($147) with RSI at 57 (neutral), but the dual resistance overhead is a concern.

Despite P/E of 221x, Rule of 40 at 127%, 60% revenue growth, and $11.2B backlog support fundamentals. Given the technically weak zone, dollar-cost averaging is more appropriate than aggressive buying. Maintaining Buy.

investmentVerdict.summary: The Iran war $200B budget is a structural positive for Palantir. Gotham, Maven, and ShipOS are all direct beneficiaries. P/E of 221x is a burden, but Rule of 40 at 127%, 60% revenue growth acceleration, and $11.2B backlog provide support. However, trading below both 50-day and 200-day SMAs signals technical weakness, so dollar-cost averaging on pullbacks is more appropriate than aggressive buying.

sources.description: Pentagon $200B war budget request, Iran ground war escalation

sources.description: Palantir vs CrowdStrike AI beneficiary comparison analysis
